headline: Maternal Mortality Ratio Rises to 28 Per 100,000 Live Births
context: Malaysia's maternal mortality ratio increased from 23.3 in 2020 to 28 per 100,000 live births in 2025.

cards.big: Maternal mortality rose from 23.3 to 28 per 100,000 live births between 2020 and 2025
cards.sub: Malaysia is moving backwards on a key health indicator after decades of progress

cards.big: Post-partum hemorrhage accounts for 35% of maternal deaths with delayed referral as key factor
cards.sub: Rural clinics lack blood products and emergency surgical capability

cards.big: Maternal mortality among Orang Asli women is 4 times the national rate at 112 per 100,000
cards.sub: Indigenous women face compounding barriers of distance language and cultural sensitivity

cards.big: Sri Lanka reduced maternal mortality to 29 per 100,000 through universal midwifery coverage
cards.sub: Every village has a trained midwife linked to a functioning referral system

cards.big: Rising maternal death in a middle-income country signals systemic healthcare failure not poverty
Should every rural clinic within 2 hours of a hospital have emergency obstetric capability
